elements.title: Pudgy
elements.contents: ... that comedian Pudgy (pictured) was often referred to as the "female Don Rickles"?

elements.title: Observatorio 1873
elements.contents: ... that Mexico's Observatorio 1873 began as a military lookout and observatory, and now includes a whale museum, an iguana sanctuary and a zip line?

elements.title: a Hindu wife of the Muslim ruler of Awadh
elements.contents: ... that a Hindu wife of the Muslim ruler of Awadh is often linked to a festival honoring Lord Hanuman on Tuesdays?

elements.title: Supreme Court of Canada set aside an election
elements.contents: ... that the Supreme Court of Canada set aside an election in a federal electoral district in Quebec after one postal vote was not counted?

elements.title: Shirakami Fubuki
elements.contents: ... that Shirakami Fubuki introduced Hololive to global audiences with a drumming showdown?

elements.title: Orleans Parish Criminal Courts Building
elements.contents: ... that the Neoclassical and Art Deco sections of the Orleans Parish Criminal Courts Building were designed by its architect to represent the elegance and the severity of the law?

elements.title: The Anatomy of Evil
elements.contents: ... that, in his book The Anatomy of Evil, forensic psychiatrist Michael Stone writes that he suffered from a nightmare after interviewing serial killer Tommy Lynn Sells in Texas?

elements.title: Lai Ka-ying
elements.contents: ... that Lai Ka-ying is the first Hongkonger in space, but not the first astronaut born in Hong Kong?

elements.title: a fighting video game
elements.contents: ... that to win a fighting video game, a player must bark loudly and frequently?
